title: Call for Applications: Small Grants Scheme for heritage-related projects led by civil society in EU candidate and neighbouring countries
content:
Opening date: 20 July – Closing date: 20 September 2026

The EU-funded European Heritage Hub aims to empower heritage stakeholders from candidate and neighbouring countries of the European Union to become active protagonists of the wider European heritage movement, thereby enhancing the ever-closer relationship between these countries and the European Union. As part of this mission, the Hub is launching the second edition of its Small Grants Scheme, with support from the ALIPH Foundation.

Civil society from 11 neighbouring countries of the European Union (Albania, Armenia, Azerbaijan, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Georgia, Kosovo*, Moldova, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Serbia and Ukraine) are eligible to apply.

  • Building the capacity of civil society;
  • Enhancing the key role of cultural heritage for the triple transformation (green, digital and social);
  • Strengthening democracy, the rule of law and other fundamental rights and values;
  • Building peace and stability, through enhancing intercultural and interreligious dialogue and reconciliation.
snippet: Opening date: 20 July – Closing date: 20 September 2026
creator: Liv Martin
